# @zakkster/lite-headless / accordion

Headless accordion primitive. N items, each with a trigger + panel.
State is reactive; controllable via signal or attribute.

## API

createAccordion({
    type:        "single" | "multiple",   // default single
    collapsible: boolean,                  // single-mode only; default false
    defaultValue,                          // string | null for single, array for multiple
    value?:      Signal,                   // controlled mode
    onValueChange: (value, reason) => void,
})

  attachRoot(el)
  attachItem(el, key)
  attachTrigger(el, key, { disabled?: boolean })
  attachPanel(el, key)

  value()                       // string | null for single, string[] for multiple
  isOpen(key)
  setValue(v, reason?)
  toggle(key, reason?)
  open(key, reason?) | close(key, reason?)
  setDisabled(key, flag)
  focusFirst() | focusLast() | focusKey(key)
  destroy()

## Modes

- "single" + collapsible:false  (default)
    Exactly one open. Click on open trigger is a no-op.
- "single" + collapsible:true
    At most one open. Click on open trigger closes it (value -> null).
- "multiple"
    Any subset open. Each trigger click toggles its panel.

## Keyboard

Per WAI-ARIA APG accordion pattern (different from tabs):
- ALL triggers in the tab sequence
- ArrowDown / ArrowUp  -- next / previous trigger (wraps, skips disabled)
- Home / End           -- first / last enabled trigger
- Enter / Space        -- activates (native button behavior)

## ARIA

- Root:      data-orientation="vertical", data-accordion-type
- Item:      data-accordion-item, data-accordion-key
- Trigger:   data-accordion-trigger, aria-expanded, aria-controls=<panelId>,
             data-open (boolean)
- Panel:     data-accordion-panel, role="region",
             aria-labelledby=<triggerId>, data-open (boolean)

## Animation contract

The primitive does NOT toggle `hidden` on panels (which would prevent
animation). It writes `data-open` only. CSS owns the animation.

Two recommended patterns:

### 1. interpolate-size (Chrome 129+/Safari 18+)

```css
[data-accordion-panel] {
    height: 0; overflow: hidden;
    transition: height 200ms;
    interpolate-size: allow-keywords;
}
[data-accordion-panel][data-open] { height: auto; }
```

### 2. grid-template-rows (universal)

```css
[data-accordion-panel] {
    display: grid; grid-template-rows: 0fr;
    transition: grid-template-rows 200ms;
}
[data-accordion-panel] > * { overflow: hidden; }
[data-accordion-panel][data-open] { grid-template-rows: 1fr; }
```

### 3. No animation

```css
[data-accordion-panel][:not([data-open])] { display: none; }
```

## Custom element wrapper

```html
<lite-accordion type="single" collapsible value="billing">
    <div data-accordion-item="overview">
        <button data-accordion-trigger="overview">Overview</button>
        <div data-accordion-panel="overview">...</div>
    </div>
    <div data-accordion-item="settings">
        <button data-accordion-trigger="settings">Settings</button>
        <div data-accordion-panel="settings">...</div>
    </div>
    <div data-accordion-item="billing" data-disabled>
        <button data-accordion-trigger="billing">Billing</button>
        <div data-accordion-panel="billing">...</div>
    </div>
</lite-accordion>
```

The `value` attribute:
- single mode: a string key, or `""` for none open
- multiple mode: comma-separated keys (`"a,b"`), or JSON array
  (`'["a","b"]'`) when a key contains a comma

Dispatches `CustomEvent('valuechange', { detail: { value, reason } })`.
Mirrors active key(s) to the host's `value` attribute on every change.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
